title: Onkel Toms Hytte
summaries: "Onkel Toms Hytte" by Harriet Beecher Stowe is an anti-slavery novel published in 1852. The story centers on Uncle Tom, a long-suffering enslaved man whose experiences reveal the brutal realities of slavery in America. Written to expose slavery's horrors while promoting Christian love as a solution, the novel became a bestseller that profoundly shaped public attitudes. Its impact was so significant that it's credited with helping fuel the abolitionist movement and laying groundwork for the Civil War, making it a landmark in protest literature.
